children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A civil action against a vaccine manufacturer for damages for a vaccine-related injury or death associated with the administration of a vaccine after , which is not barred by shall be tried in three stages.October 1, 1988section 300aa–11(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Liability
children.label: b Liability
children.text: The first stage of such a civil action shall be held to determine if a vaccine manufacturer is liable under .section 300aa–22 of this title

children.heading: General damages
children.label: c General damages
children.text: The second stage of such a civil action shall be held to determine the amount of damages (other than punitive damages) a vaccine manufacturer found to be liable under shall be required to pay.section 300aa–22 of this title

If sought by the plaintiff, the third stage of such an action shall be held to determine the amount of punitive damages a vaccine manufacturer found to be liable under shall be required to pay.section 300aa–22 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: fraud or intentional and wrongful withholding of information from the Secretary during any phase of a proceeding for approval of the vaccine under ,section 262 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: intentional and wrongful withholding of information relating to the safety or efficacy of the vaccine after its approval, or

children.label: C
children.text: other criminal or illegal activity relating to the safety and effectiveness of vaccines,

children.heading: Evidence
children.label: e Evidence
children.text: In any stage of a civil action, the Vaccine Injury Table, any finding of fact or conclusion of law of the United States Court of Federal Claims or a special master in a proceeding on a petition filed under and the final judgment of the United States Court of Federal Claims and subsequent appellate review on such a petition shall not be admissible.section 300aa–11 of this title
